project_type: Python Streamlit web application - AI text humanization pipeline
build_system: pip with requirements.txt, no Makefile or formal build system
test_framework: No formal test suite - validation via import checks and manual testing
build_command: pip install -r requirements.txt && python -c "from humanizer.pipeline import HumanizationPipeline; print('OK')"
test_command: python -c "from humanizer.pipeline import HumanizationPipeline; from humanizer.config import INTENSITY_PROFILES; p = HumanizationPipeline(intensity=4); print('Pipeline OK')"
verification_instructions: 1. Run import validation of all modules. 2. Verify pipeline instantiation at all intensity levels. 3. Run streamlit app in headless mode to check for errors.
setup_instructions: pip install -r requirements.txt && python -m spacy download en_core_web_sm && python -c "import nltk; nltk.download('wordnet'); nltk.download('omw-1.4')"
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET - full network access available. spaCy en_core_web_sm model required. NLTK WordNet data required.
contribution_requirements: No formal CONTRIBUTING.md. Code should be clean Python, maintain the existing class-based stage architecture.
key_patterns: Each stage is a class with __init__(aggression, seed) and process(text) method. Pipeline orchestrates stages in order. Config.py holds all constants. Streaming is handled via generator methods in LLMRewriter.
directory_structure: Root: app.py (Streamlit UI), requirements.txt. humanizer/: config.py (constants), pipeline.py (orchestrator), stage_structural.py (stage 1), stage_lexical.py (stage 2), stage_llm_rewrite.py (stage 3), stage_perplexity.py (stage 4), stage_postprocess.py (stage 5), text_analysis.py (analytics).
